---
title: "Visualizations"
bibliography: references.bib
sidebar: example-sidebar
filters:
  - jsxgraph/jsxgraph
---

## Interactive Plot

Drag the point along the x-axis to read off values of $\sin(x)$.
The plot is rendered with the [JSXGraph Quarto extension](https://github.com/jsxgraph/jsxgraph-quarto) [@jsxgraph].

```jsxgraph
var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(BOARDID, {
    boundingbox: [-7, 1.8, 7, -1.8],
    axis: true,
    keepaspectratio: false,
    showNavigation: false
});

board.create('functiongraph', [Math.sin], {
    strokeColor: '#2196F3',
    strokeWidth: 2.5,
    name: 'sin(x)'
});

var xAxis = board.defaultAxes.x;

var p = board.create('glider', [1.571, 0, xAxis], {
    name: 'x',
    size: 5,
    fillColor: '#E91E63',
    strokeColor: '#c2185b'
});

var q = board.create('point', [
    function() { return p.X(); },
    function() { return Math.sin(p.X()); }
], {
    name: function() { return 'sin(x) ≈ ' + Math.sin(p.X()).toFixed(3); },
    size: 5,
    fillColor: '#4CAF50',
    strokeColor: '#388e3c',
    fixed: true
});

board.create('segment', [p, q], {
    strokeColor: '#4CAF50',
    strokeWidth: 1.5,
    dash: 2
});
```

## Diagram

How does Quarto turn a `.qmd` file into a finished document?

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[".qmd File"] --> B{"Quarto"}
    B --> C["🌐 HTML Website"]
    B --> D["📄 PDF"]
    B --> E["📊 Presentation"]
    style B fill:#4CAF50,color:#fff,stroke:#388e3c
    style A fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#90caf9
```

→ The code that generated the plot above lives on the [Code page](ep_code.qmd).
